mod.amdium.name: Amdium
mod.amdium.desc: AMD GPU-optimized chunk renderer using Multi-Draw Indirect and compute-shader culling
amdium.config.title: Amdium Settings
amdium.config.enableMDI.tooltip: Combines all chunk draw calls into a single Multi-Draw Indirect call.
amdium.config.vertexPoolMB.tooltip: Size of the global vertex buffer. More = fewer reallocations at high render distances.
amdium.config.enableComputeCulling.toolt: Frustum culling on GPU via compute shader. Removes invisible chunks without CPU load.
amdium.config.workgroupSize.tooltip: Workgroup size for the compute shader (must be a power of 2). Optimal is 64 on AMD (one wavefront).
amdium.config.enableIndirectCount.toolti: GPU reads drawCount from a parameter buffer. Eliminates CPU readback pipeline stalls.
amdium.config.enableInteropComputeCullin: When Embeddium is installed: a compute shader culls the region (frustum + fog) and compacts draw commands on the GPU; glMultiDrawElementsIndirectCount reads count from the GPU — zero readback (Nvidium-style). Requires GL 4.3 + ARB_indirect_parameters.
amdium.config.enablePersistentMapping.to: Persistent mapped staging buffer for zero-copy chunk uploads.
amdium.config.framesInFlight.tooltip: Number of frames queued on the GPU (frame pacing). 3 = triple buffering. More = smoother but more VRAM.
amdium.config.disableVanillaLayer.toolti: Fully cancel vanilla renderChunkLayer. Set false to render MDI alongside vanilla (for debugging).
amdium.config.showCulled.tooltip: Show wireframe for chunks culled by frustum. Slows down rendering!
amdium.config.logFrameStats.tooltip: Log per-frame stats: draw calls, culled chunks, GPU time.
amdium.config.status.disabled: Mod Disabled (Your GPU does not support MDI)
